statement.summary: This article identifies military threats to air operations and airports against the backdrop of the Russia-Ukraine conflict from 2014 to 2026 and Iran's military actions following the US and Israeli attacks. These conflicts demonstrate the new threats wars pose to civil aviation and the changing approach of politicians and military commanders to combat strategies and attacks on civilian targets. Civil aviation and passenger aircraft have long been attractive targets for terrorist attacks, but recently, intentional military aggression has been used to achieve concessions or even victory in war. The article cites cases of passenger aircraft shootdowns and attacks on airports.The aim of this article is to identify military threats affecting the security of air operations and airports in light

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The axiom identifies empirically documented military threats (shootdowns, airport attacks in Ukraine/Iran contexts) — TRUE component — while simultaneously engaging normative claims about 'security' and 'threats' that depend on geopolitical framing and strategic interpretation — contested/perspective-dependent component. The factual observations coexist with unstable categorizations of what constitutes a 'threat' vs. 'legitimate military action'.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 戦争の論理が民間航空の安全性を食い尽くし、許容される危害の積分が増えていく。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 上昇する航空機が領空の十字を切り裂く—境界線と軍事的意図が交差する危険な空間。
